articles.title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
Plato aces space-like tests
articles.summary: The European Space Agency Plato mission has successfully completed a series of tough tests under space‑like conditions. With this accomplishment, the spacecraft is on track to lift off in early 2027 and begin its search for terrestrial planets.

articles.title: SOLIS100 isolation study begins in Germany
articles.summary: Six volunteers have stepped into a sealed habitat in Cologne, Germany, marking the start of SOLIS100, a new human isolation study led by the European Space Agency (ESA) and conducted by the German Aerospace Center (DLR).

articles.title: Smoke Shrouds Northern Thailand
articles.summary: Seasonal fires have darkened skies over Southeast Asia.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 25 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg SFB
articles.summary: The mission is the 40th launch of the year from SpaceX, dedicated to flying its broadband internet satellites. Liftoff of the Starlink 17-14 mission is scheduled for 7:55 p.m. PDT (10:55 p.m. EDT / 0255 UTC).

articles.title: NASA’s 777 Aircraft Returns Home with Science Flights on the Horizon
articles.summary: NASA’s Boeing 777 has returned to the agency’s fleet after undergoing heavy structural modifications as it transforms from a giant passenger plane into the agency’s next-generation airborne science laboratory. After a check flight and a three-hour transit from Waco, the aircraft returned to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on April 22. Since January […]

articles.title: You want your Moon landings in HDTV? So does NASA. Here's how it's happening.
articles.summary: "You just push this button, and in three hours, you're counting photons."

articles.title: NASA Targets Early September for Roman Space Telescope Launch
articles.summary: NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope team now is targeting as soon as early September 2026 for launch, ahead of the agency’s commitment to flight no later than May 2027. “Roman’s accelerated development is a true success story of what we can achieve when public investment, institutional expertise, and private enterprise come together to take […]

articles.title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon to Discuss Upcoming Launch, Mission
articles.summary: NASA will host a news conference at 1:45 p.m. EDT Wednesday, April 29, from the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston to preview astronaut Anil Menon’s upcoming mission to the International Space Station. Watch the news conference live on NASA’s YouTube channel. Learn how to watch NASA content through a variety of online platforms, including […]
